title: Simplification of the digital legislative framework (Digital Omnibus)

scope_and_definition_of_digital_services.description: The scope and definition of 'digital services' or 'digital legislative framework' that this omnibus simplification will apply to, determining which existing laws are affected and which entities fall under its purview.
scope_and_definition_of_digital_services.salience: Industry stakeholders seek clarity and predictability regarding which of their operations are covered, while civil society fears over-reach or carve-outs that could weaken protections. Member States may also contest definitions impacting national competence.

regulatory_burden_vs_consumer_protection.description: The inherent tension between reducing regulatory compliance costs for businesses through simplification and maintaining or strengthening the level of protection for consumers, users, and fundamental rights.
regulatory_burden_vs_consumer_protection.salience: Industry groups, especially SMEs, will lobby for significant reductions in red tape and compliance costs, while consumer organisations and civil liberties advocates will push to ensure that simplification does not dilute existing safeguards or create new vulnerabilities for users.

harmonisation_level_and_member_state_fle.description: The degree to which the 'Digital Omnibus' aims for full harmonisation across the EU, potentially limiting Member States' ability to implement specific national rules or adaptations for digital services.
harmonisation_level_and_member_state_fle.salience: Some Member States will seek to preserve national specificities and regulatory flexibility, while industries operating cross-border will strongly advocate for full harmonisation to reduce fragmentation and legal uncertainty across the single market.

enforcement_framework_and_regulatory_pow.description: Potential changes to the structure, powers, and cooperation mechanisms of national competent authorities and the European Commission in enforcing the simplified digital rules.
enforcement_framework_and_regulatory_pow.salience: National regulatory bodies may resist changes that alter their mandates or resources, while the Commission might seek to enhance central oversight. Industry desires predictable and consistent enforcement, and civil society demands effective redress and accountability mechanisms.

data_governance_and_interoperability_imp.description: How the simplification affects rules concerning data sharing, access, portability, and interoperability across various digital services and potentially different sectors.
data_governance_and_interoperability_imp.salience: Dominant tech platforms may resist mandates that compel data sharing or enhance interoperability, while challenger firms, data-intensive industries, and privacy advocates will push for greater data access, user control, and data protection safeguards.

impact_on_smes_and_startups.description: Whether the simplification genuinely benefits Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) and start-ups by reducing their administrative burden, or if it creates new, disproportionate compliance costs or inadvertently favours larger incumbents.
impact_on_smes_and_startups.salience: SME associations will lobby for specific exemptions, lighter regimes, or tailored support. Larger corporations might resist such differentiations, arguing for a level playing field, while national governments will seek to ensure benefits for their local digital economies.

future_proofing_and_innovation_incentive.description: How the simplified framework addresses emerging technologies (e.g., AI, Web3, metaverse) and whether its provisions foster innovation within the EU or inadvertently create new regulatory hurdles.
future_proofing_and_innovation_incentive.salience: Tech innovators and investors will advocate for flexible, non-prescriptive rules, and regulatory sandboxes. Civil society and academics will push for robust ethical guidelines and risk mitigation for new technologies, ensuring responsible innovation.

transparency_and_accountability_requirem.description: Potential modifications to existing transparency obligations for digital platforms, including algorithmic transparency, content moderation policies, and reporting requirements.
transparency_and_accountability_requirem.salience: Civil society organisations and researchers will advocate for strengthened transparency to ensure platform accountability and user rights. Digital platforms will seek to reduce perceived excessive reporting burdens and protect trade secrets related to their algorithms.
